mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window or positive return frequency drops below 50% during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeds 0.2 percentage points, signaling deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive equity valuations over a quarterly horizon through improved corporate earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail and housing data coincide with negative 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Positive industrial production growth fails to generate positive 20-day SPX returns during early-cycle phases, with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent five trading days.
failure_condition: Inflation prints above 0.3% MoM are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 65% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly nonfarm payroll additions above 150,000 sustain consumer spending growth and corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: Payroll growth above 150k correlates with negative 60-day SPX returns in > 50% of months, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly GDP expansion and rising retail sales confirm synchronized demand across production and consumption channels, driving positive equity returns over a one-month horizon through earnings revision cycles.
failure_condition: Positive GDP and retail sales growth coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of overlapping observations.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production signal a supply-side contraction that precedes earnings downgrades, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive industrial production declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 55% of cases, invalidating the recessionary signal.
